item.description:
Well, he doesn't want to be a king: he wants to be a dictator. . . We'll, he's already a dick.
Sorry for the AI narrator. I would have reduced it myself if I could have. The message was important, not the messenger.

17 october https://thehill.com/homenews/house/5560485-jeffries-legal-action-republicans-grijalva/
https://www.abc15.com/news/local-news/with-lawsuit-looming-house-speaker-continues-to-refuse-to-swear-in-congresswoman-elect-adelita-grijalva

item.title: Why are trans people rights not a polarizing issue in Australia? They were never used as political points
item.description:
https://lens.monash.edu/@politics-society/2025/10/17/1387926/the-power-of-allies-in-safeguarding-the-rights-of-trans-people-in-australia
• Strong majority support for non-discrimination protections A 2023 survey by Ipsos found that in Australia 79 % agreed that transgender people should be protected from discrimination in employment, housing and access to business. Ipsos https://www.ipsos.com/en-au/transgender-anti-discrimination A survey by Equality Australia found that 78 % of Australians believe trans people deserve the same rights and protections as other Australians (57 % “strongly agree”) and only 7 % actively disagree. https://equalityaustralia.org.au/new-research-shows-overwhelming-support-among-australians Another poll found that 86 % of Australians agreed or strongly agreed that government and opposition should not politicise trans issues for political gain. Equality Australia These figures show relatively broad consensus compared to what you might expect in a highly polarised culture-war environment. • Commentary supporting “no major culture‐war traction” An article in Crikey titled “Read the room, politicians: no one wants a transgender rights culture war” observes that despite some agitation, polling shows most Australians supportive of trans rights and “you wouldn’t know it from our political discourse”. Crikey https://www.crikey.com.au/2023/06/23/transgender-rights-culture-war-australia/ A summary of the legal situation: rights for transgender people are recognised under federal and state/territory laws in Australia. Wikipedia https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transgender_rights_in_Australia So not only is there survey evidence of broad public support, but there is commentary that the issue simply hasn’t become the kind of wedge-issue it is in some other countries. West, you have been made.
item.dc:creator: d14z3p4m

item.title: Thank You Dublin – Thank You London – The UK Show Of Support For The USA's "No Kings" Protests
item.description:
Hundreds have gathered outside the US embassy in London for a protest against the fascist Trump regime.
London
Frogs Across The Pond – Dublin
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Just a Number
item.description: Edit 2: It's amazing that there are a few shitheads on here that are blaming troops for the politicians wars A little backstory. I signed up in 2008 when the economy failed. There were no trade schools, no job opportunities. Nothing that could save me from the inevitable homelessness. I was going to have. My father was one of those assholes that was kicking me out without any help. I had no family, no safety not to fall on. But there were two wars brought on by Bush. There are a few toxic people down below who really think that it's the fault of people like me who had no options That made the choices that sent us to Afghanistan and Iraq. Often times, I find these people are one of two categories. Most times, people who couldn't muster the courage to serve their community, let alone their country. Often times, that don't even participate in the systems that could fix things, like voting. A lot of tankies, as we call them, the same people who abstain their vote for Kamala Harris because of Joe Biden's handling of Gaza. Or the alternative, the far right looms that everybody hates known as MAGA. That one's pretty self-explanatory. The things that fucked me up the most that I can share are seeing that religious extremists went into places far before we got there and were abusing the children and women because they wanted to have some semblance of a normal life. I remember two little girls that we investigated that were hit with Boric acid by a dude on a motorcycle because they weren't supposed to be going to school. It was my intelligence gathering and scrutinizing that led to us finding the person that did it to sell the operated out of and the people that coerced that sell and to do in horrible things. In the end, in West Afghanistan, we ended up interdicting and stopping people from attacking women. Now, this isn't saying the middle theory hasn't fucked up and done horrible things. That is not what I'm saying. But... At the root of it, just like now, it was a Republican politician that pushed us into the conflicts for their own inflated egos and reasons. I wish I was not 35 years old with a broken body that could have opportunities to live a normal life. I could barely eat normally. I could barely function physically on a semi regular basis. I by no means am special, but do not deserve the ire of the far left who contribute nothing at all, or the far right who are attacking and destroying everything. But hey, continue to push people who are already not doing well and pushing that upper and upper upwards. Good on you for being that type of person. Fp Edit: I appreciate the kind of people in the comments. I have frankly given up hope on ever feeling better or making progress. I don't think that option is on the table for us anymore. The way I'm looking at it will survive as long as I can and then die and be forgotten. They never will care. And you can't go to the VA for help anymore. The current administration has villainized mental health and has using it to target people. Sincerely, a 100% service connected, prominent, and total disabled Army combat vet. For those of you out there that support us, just want to say thank you. If you want to help me out. https://buymeacoffee.com/gridsquares
item.dc:creator: SquaresNews
